New York Dietitian License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
No Interstate Compact

How to Transfer Your Dietitian License to New York

There is no active interstate compact covering dietitian practice in New York. To work in New York, apply for licensure by endorsement through New York State Board for Dietetics and Nutrition.

Endorsement fee
$294
Processing time
~6 Weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Not listed
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Complete Education & Exam Requirements

    Earn a Master's degree, complete at least 1,000 hours of ACEND-accredited supervised practice, and pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) national examination.

  2. Take the Child Abuse Identification Course

    New York State requires all healthcare professionals to complete state-approved coursework in identifying and reporting child abuse before applying for certification.

  3. Apply Online via NYSED

    Submit your application for a new CDN certification through the Office of the Professions online portal and pay the combined $294 application and initial registration fee.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$294
Processing time
~6 Weeks
Renewal fee
$179
Renewal cycle
Every 3 years

Required Documents

  • Official College Transcripts: Must be sent directly from your degree-granting university to the NYSED.
  • CDR Certification Verification: Proof of active registration sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Child Abuse Training Certificate: Certificate of completion for the mandatory NYS child abuse identification coursework.

Board Contact

Board
New York State Board for Dietetics and Nutrition
Department
NYSED Office of the Professions
Phone
(518) 474-3817
Email
dietbd@nysed.gov

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $179. Renewal cycle: Every 3 years.

New York Dietitian Reciprocity FAQ

How much does a New York dietitian license cost?

The total initial cost is $294, which covers the NYSED's $115 application fee and the $179 fee for your first three-year registration period.

Do I need a master's degree to become a CDN in New York?

Yes. As of January 1, 2024, a minimum of a master's degree is required by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) to sit for the national RDN exam, which serves as the exam basis for NY state certification.

Is continuing education required to renew a New York CDN license?

The NYSED does not mandate additional state-level continuing education for renewal, but you must maintain your active CDR registration, which requires completing 75 hours of continuing professional education every 5 years.

Does New York have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in New York. To work in New York, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to New York?

Most endorsement applications take ~6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in New York while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
